title: Delivery Tags, Acknowledgements, and Redelivery · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for delivery tags, acknowledgements, and redelivery and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a same-channel terminal delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Transfer ownership only after the intended durable effect, transactional inbox record, or safely repeatable handoff succeeds; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Ack Race Single Owner: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7610 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; reject stale connection and channel generations; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ack Race Single Owner.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ack Race Single Owner, keep Ack Race Single Owner connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ack Race Single Owner, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Ack Race Single Owner precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ack Race Single Owner, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7610 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce same-generation exactly-one terminal delivery decision after durable effect; reject stale connection and channel generations; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ack Race Single Owner, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Calling ack twice is idempotent and safely ignored by RabbitMQ. In Go, delivery tags are globally reusable after reconnect.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
